data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer) vs WHOLE roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

Re-verified the 7 cross-child interaction modes (high-confidence, benign-reading filter), confirming against code:

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned (parent What/Scope). Search confirms zero `managed_refs` matches in tree → parent builds it; this child only READS. No undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.
2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree: peer ref present + locally absent ⇒ CLEAR/REMOVE iff ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, no inbound-remove dual. ba2c matches 908c and parent. CLEAN.
3. Dependency gap — ba2c depends on the parent primitive/gate; both children carry parent_id b4d4 (the builder), supplying ordering. CLEAN.
4. Scope overlap — ba2c = PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`, `emit_detach_clear` branch; verified outbound_differ.py:400-414, `result["parent"] = None`); 908c = LINK path (`_diff_links`, acli delete-relationship, batch_dispatch link REMOVE). Disjoint code paths confirmed. Shared gate = common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic. CLEAN.
5. Ordering violation — independent consumers; no inter-child ordering dependency. CLEAN.
6. Consumer impact — both extend same-pass inbound suppression on DISJOINT keys (ba2c: scalar parent→parent_id; 908c: link-add echo set). No cross-interference. CLEAN.
7. Residual references — alias "safe-luge-nog" = b4d4 confirmed; ba2c's own alias tan-elbow-mica. No stale reference. CLEAN.

The Jira "Blocks" direction-collapse note (inbound_differ.py:492-502 recovers direction from inward/outward, vs direction-keyed managed_refs) affects sibling 908c only; ba2c's parent ref is single-direction `(parent, target)`, unambiguous — NOT a cross-child contradiction with ba2c.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).
